message:
feat(glibc_internal): port __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini no-op stubs (bd-g1o7q)

Adds the glibc startup-stub symbols that older glibc versions
(<2.34) emitted as part of program-startup code: `_start` would
call `__libc_csu_init(argc, argv, envp)` before main and
`__libc_csu_fini()` after exit to drive `.init_array` /
`.fini_array` constructors. Modern glibc inlines these into
_start, but statically-linked binaries built with older
toolchains still reference the symbols and fail at link time
without them.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust no-op stubs. Our runtime
drives constructors and atexit handlers via separate machinery
(__cxa_atexit / __cxa_finalize plus the existing startup
plumbing), so the csu shells have nothing to do.

Tests: 3 abi integration tests covering: (a) init with NULL
args resolves and returns; (b) init with realistic argc/argv/
envp resolves and returns; (c) fini resolves and returns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verified via cargo test conformance_diff_ftw_nftw — 6 passing including new diff_nftw_nonexistent_dir.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","ftw"]}

Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:32.536661964Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:28:35.998745793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:28:35.996555015Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-fzvyj","title":"PORT: strenvisx/strsenvisx (NetBSD env-aware vis(3) variants)","description":"Port the NetBSD vis(3) env-aware variants: strenvisx(dst, src, srclen, flags, cerr_ptr) and strsenvisx(dst, dlen, src, srclen, flags, extra, cerr_ptr). These are extended forms of strvisx/strsvisx that additionally consult the VIS_OPTIONS environment variable for comma-separated flag names (e.g. "VIS_OCTAL,VIS_NL,VIS_TAB,VIS_CSTYLE,VIS_SP,VIS_NOSLASH,VIS_HTTPSTYLE,VIS_GLOB,VIS_HTTP1808,VIS_MIMESTYLE,VIS_NOLOCALE,VIS_SAFE,VIS_DQ,VIS_SHELL,VIS_META") and OR the parsed bits into the caller-supplied flags. They also write a per-call character-set error indicator into *cerr_ptr (always 0 for our byte-stream-safe port). Implementation: a small parser maps known token names to existing VIS_* constants, ignores unrecognized tokens; abi shims read VIS_OPTIONS via libc::getenv and apply the OR-merge before delegating to the existing encoder. Tests: env-var bits are honored, NULL/empty env-var is a no-op, unrecognized tokens are ignored, cerr_ptr writes 0, NULL cerr_ptr tolerated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:44:50.976787675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:49:40.109783228Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:49:40.107811745Z","close_reason":"shipped: strenvisx + strsenvisx with VIS_OPTIONS env-var flag merge + parse_vis_options core helper + cerr_ptr writeback + 5 core tests + 8 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-g1o7q","title":"PORT: __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini (glibc startup stub no-ops)","description":"Port the glibc startup-stub symbols __libc_csu_init(int argc, char **argv, char **envp) and __libc_csu_fini(void). These were emitted by older glibc versions (<2.34) as part of the program-startup code generated by gcc/ld; the entry point calls __libc_csu_init before main and __libc_csu_fini after exit to drive .init_array / .fini_array constructors. Modern glibc inlines these into _start, but statically-linked binaries built with older toolchains still reference the symbols and fail at link time without them. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust no-op stubs — our runtime drives constructors / __cxa_atexit handlers via separate machinery, so the csu shells have nothing to do. Tests verify the symbols resolve and the calls return without panic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:23:23.495380822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:23:23.495380822Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g1wfq","title":"PORT: __cxa_pure_virtual (Itanium C++ ABI pure-virtual stub)","description":"Port the Itanium C++ ABI `__cxa_pure_virtual()` entry point. C++ compilers populate vtable slots for pure virtual functions with a pointer to this symbol; it gets invoked at runtime if a caller dispatches through an under-construction or partially-destructed object whose vtable still points to a pure-virtual stub. Per Itanium ABI / glibc convention, the implementation writes "pure virtual method called" + "terminate called without an active exception" diagnostics to stderr and calls abort(). Without this symbol, every C++ binary linked against our libc fails at link time with an undefined reference. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" fn that writes the diagnostic via libc::write to fd 2 and invokes libc::abort(). Tests cover the symbol resolves (via fork+execute test that calls __cxa_pure_virtual in the child and verifies the child terminates via SIGABRT with the expected stderr message).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:28:24.233962059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:32:04.891791757Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:32:04.889775961Z","close_reason":"shipped: __cxa_pure_virtual Itanium C++ ABI stub writing diagnostic + abort + 1 fork-based abi test verifying SIGABRT termination, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g28e","title":"Execute stdlib_sort fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade stdlib_sort conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:47:59.748287795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:01.287010754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:01.284058581Z","close_reason":"Upgraded stdlib_sort conformance coverage to execute each fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g2bd5","title":"[CRITICAL] glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption under default parallelism (free(): invalid size)","description":"## Symptom

`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` aborts with:

```
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
...
process didn't exit successfully: ...glibc_internal_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)
```

Reproduced on two independent workers (vmi1149989, vmi1156319) with current main tip 7c9b3127 on 2026-04-22.

## Key diagnostic

The same binary with `--test-threads=1` **passes all 172 tests cleanly** (finishes in 0.04s). Corruption only manifests when the default rayon/cargo test parallelism is enabled. That narrows the bug to:

- a data race on a heap-allocated global / thread-local
- two tests freeing the same allocation through different paths
- or a reallocation that trusts a size field modified concurrently

## Location hint

Failure in the two runs landed near these test names (the test that actually freed the corrupt pointer is not necessarily the one that emitted the abort — whichever thread allocated last is what glibc trips on):

run 1 (vmi1149989): last 'ok' line was `resolv_context_native_shim_reuses_tls_context_and_preserves_errno`, then `free(): invalid size`.

run 2 (vmi1156319): last 'ok' line was `test_idna_from_dns_null_input`, then `free(): invalid size`.

Cluster of tests running around the failure: TLS dtors (`test_call_tls_dtors_*`), IDNA (`test_idna_*`), resolv context shims (`resolv_context_native_shim_*`, `resolv_context_override_*`). These all touch thread-local state / TLS dtor lists and share a registry.

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=2` to get a glibc backtrace pointing at the freeing site.
2. Audit the TLS-dtor list, resolv-context override pool, and IDNA allocation paths for any `Box::from_raw` / `free` on a pointer whose size metadata is modified elsewhere without a lock.
3. Add `#[ignore]` or `serial_test::serial` gate on the implicated tests only after the root cause is understood (applying `serial` prematurely masks the bug instead of fixing it).

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace` from completing cleanly — the very first workspace-wide test abort halts CI before later crates (frankenlibc-core, frankenlibc-membrane, frankenlibc-harness, frankenlibc-bench) ever run. Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE pass.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` reproduces reliably on two distinct workers.
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test -- --test-threads=1` passes (172/172, 0.04s).
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.

Actual compilation via gcc has a -plugin linker argument issue (ld gets wrong args). Tracked separately.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-s55cu","title":"PORT: __libc_fatal (glibc internal noreturn fatal-error helper)","description":"Port the glibc internal `void __libc_fatal(const char *message)` noreturn helper. ld.so internals, pthread setup paths, and the malloc heap-corruption detector call this when something is unrecoverable: write `message` to stderr then abort. Without it, statically-linked glibc subsystems fail at link time. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" fn marked `-> !` that writes the NUL-terminated message via libc::write to fd 2 and calls libc::abort(). NULL message yields a fallback "fatal: unknown error\n" to avoid faulting on the diagnostic. Tests: fork-based test verifies SIGABRT termination and that the diagnostic appears on the captured stderr.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:16:18.490496608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:16:18.490496608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Without it, statically-linked glibc subsystems fail at link time. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" fn marked `-> !` that writes the NUL-terminated message via libc::write to fd 2 and calls libc::abort(). NULL message yields a fallback "fatal: unknown error\n" to avoid faulting on the diagnostic. Tests: fork-based test verifies SIGABRT termination and that the diagnostic appears on the captured stderr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:16:18.490496608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:19:15.262466158Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:19:15.260397642Z","close_reason":"shipped: __libc_fatal noreturn helper writing message to stderr + abort, NULL fallback + 2 fork-based abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-s83dv","title":"fuzz_pathname harness: leaks realpath(NULL) return instead of freeing","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs:153-158 calls realpath(cs.as_ptr(), NULL), gets a malloc'd buffer, and deliberately leaks it (comment: 'leak in the fuzz target is acceptable'). LeakSanitizer flags this and exits with 77 as soon as a path bytes combination makes realpath succeed (resolving to something short, e.g. 2 bytes). Effect: short fuzz runs fail on an infrastructural leak, masking any real library-side leak. Fix: unsafe { libc::free(ret as *mut c_void) } after archetype completes. Our realpath at stdlib_abi.rs:4050 uses libc::malloc so libc::free is the right pair. Reproducer: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_pathname/leak-f413061db6eee949e03cba98bbb1b4526ec3525c","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:06:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:06:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: free realpath/canonicalize_file_name returns with libc::free in fuzz_pathname harness. Landed in 9e170061. 5.4M runs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sas1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc sigaltstack diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 1 function: sigaltstack (query current, set+disable round-trip with cross-impl readback, size-too-small ENOMEM edge). 4 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T16:45:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","sigaltstack","signal"]}
 {"id":"bd-sbt21","title":"sprintf executor returns 'unsupported printf arg value' for float %e","description":"execute_sprintf_case in frankenlibc_conformance cannot dispatch certain printf conversions (observed: sprintf_e_basic with 12345.0). Surfaced while wiring bd-12hh printf_conformance harness matrix. Those specific fixture cases are skipped in the harness-matrix tests until the executor gains broader conversion support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:00:37.882801746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:00.070271168Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:00.068275622Z","close_reason":"parse_printf_args now always routes JSON float literals to PrintfArg::Double; added PrintfArg::NullPtr + 3/4/5-arg dispatch arms for star-width, multiple-conversion, mixed-type, and consecutive-int fixtures. Remaining executor gaps tracked in bd-0u5fm/bd-luc3d/bd-vgrav/bd-qij6l via KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS. Committed a4789ab.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -8953,6 +8953,37 @@ pub unsafe extern "C" fn __libc_use_alloca(size: usize) -> c_int {
 /// paths, and the malloc heap-corruption detector when something
 /// is unrecoverable. Marked `-> !` because we never return.
 ///
+/// glibc `__libc_csu_init(argc, argv, envp)` — startup-stub
+/// no-op. Older glibc versions (<2.34) emitted this as the
+/// program-entry helper that drives `.init_array` constructors
+/// before main runs. Modern glibc inlines it into `_start`; we
+/// have no need for it because constructors are driven through
+/// our own startup machinery + the C++ `__cxa_atexit` path.
+/// We accept the symbol as a no-op so statically-linked binaries
+/// built with older toolchains still link.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// All arguments are accepted and ignored.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __libc_csu_init(
+    _argc: c_int,
+    _argv: *mut *mut c_char,
+    _envp: *mut *mut c_char,
+) {
+    // Intentionally empty.
+}
+
+/// glibc `__libc_csu_fini()` — startup-stub no-op. Companion of
+/// [`__libc_csu_init`] that older glibc (<2.34) called via
+/// `_start` to run `.fini_array` destructors after exit. Our
+/// runtime handles destructors via `__cxa_finalize`; this shell
+/// has nothing to do.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __libc_csu_fini() {
+    // Intentionally empty.
+}
+
 /// NULL `message` falls back to a generic "fatal: unknown error"
 /// diagnostic to avoid faulting on the diagnostic itself.
 ///
